label: Uncomplicated laryngitis without airway or deep-neck red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated laryngitis.
No airway symptoms, drooling, deep neck infection concern, severe throat pain, hemoptysis, neck mass, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed ENT plan.
exclusion_criteria: Airway symptoms, stridor, drooling, inability to swallow, deep neck infection concern, epiglottitis concern, severe throat pain, neck swelling, hemoptysis, or systemic illness.
Immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, voice-professional/specialist-directed plan, or prolonged/recurrent hoarseness requiring ENT evaluation.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care or ENT if symptoms persist, recur, or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports laryngitis framing, hoarseness/voice-loss symptoms, voice rest, fluids, humidified air, and avoidance of irritants. Autonomous Phase 1801-1850 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated laryngitis without airway/deep neck red flags, hemoptysis, systemic illness, high-risk host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, prolonged/recurrent hoarseness pathway, or specialist-directed ENT plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports laryngitis framing, hoarseness/voice-loss symptoms, voice rest, fluids, humidified air, and avoidance of irritants. Autonomous Phase 1801-1850 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated laryngitis without airway/deep neck red flags, hemoptysis, systemic illness, high-risk host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, prolonged/recurrent hoarseness pathway, or specialist-directed ENT plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated laryngitis
no airway symptoms, trouble breathing, drooling, neck swelling, or deep neck infection concern documented
no hemoptysis, prolonged/recurrent hoarseness, high-risk host, pregnancy, pediatric, or specialist-directed pathway
